title: Christchurch Winter School Holidays 2026 — Family Activities & Ideas
meta_desc: What to do in Christchurch these winter school holidays (27 Jun–12 Jul 2026). Antarctic Centre, ice skating, Willowbank, hot pools, free museums and costs for families.
intro: Christchurch leans into winter better than most NZ cities — crisp, frosty mornings, the Southern Alps a day trip away, and an ice rink that suits the season perfectly. The break runs Saturday 27 June to Sunday 12 July 2026, with Matariki on Friday 10 July. From the brilliant International Antarctic Centre to ice skating, the free flagship museums, Willowbank wildlife and the hot pools at Hanmer Springs, here's our family guide to a Christchurch winter holiday — with honest prices and a sample day plan.
h2_outline: Hot pools and snow within reach

sections.heading: Christchurch Winter School Holiday Dates 2026
sections.body: Christchurch schools follow the national Term 2 dates. Matariki on Friday 10 July is a public holiday inside the break, with free council events around the city — popular attractions get busy that long weekend.

sections.heading: Antarctic Adventures and Ice Skating
sections.body:
The International Antarctic Centre is the perfect winter outing — and feeling the storm room's wind chill makes the real Christchurch cold seem positively mild. There's a Hägglunds ride, little blue penguins and an aquarium too. It's a splurge at around $59/adult and $39/child, so allow a good two to three hours to get your money's worth, and book online for cheaper combo tickets.

Winter is also ice-skating season. Alpine Ice Sports Centre runs public sessions all year and is a fun, cheap morning for school-age kids and up; skate hire is included for around $20 a person.
list: International Antarctic Centre — storm room, penguins, Hägglunds ride; ~$59/adult, $39/child
Alpine Ice Sports Centre — public ice skating, ~$20/person with hire
Margaret Mahy Family Playground — biggest in the Southern Hemisphere, free even in winter
Tūranga central library — five floors of free play, maker spaces and reading nooks

sections.heading: Free Museums and Galleries
sections.body: Christchurch is generous with free attractions. Canterbury Museum (check its current redevelopment/temporary site status) and the Air Force Museum at Wigram are both free, with real aircraft kids can get close to. The Christchurch Art Gallery is free with family trails, and Riverside Market is free to wander with cheap eats for lunch.
list: Canterbury Museum — free / koha (confirm current site during redevelopment)
Air Force Museum of NZ, Wigram — free, real aircraft, flight simulators
Christchurch Art Gallery Te Puna o Waiwhetū — free, family trails
Quake City — the earthquake story, great for older kids (small entry)
Riverside Market — free to wander, warm lunch spot

sections.heading: Wildlife Parks and Gardens
sections.body: Wrap up warm and the animals are often more active in the cool. Willowbank Wildlife Reserve has a nocturnal kiwi house (a winter highlight) plus farmyard favourites, while Orana Wildlife Park is NZ's only open-range zoo with lions, giraffes and the rhino feed. The Botanic Gardens and Hagley Park are free, with the warm glasshouses a lovely escape from the cold.
list: Willowbank Wildlife Reserve — kiwi house, hands-on farmyard; ~$35/adult, $16/child
Orana Wildlife Park — open-range zoo, lions, giraffe feeding; ticketed
Christchurch Botanic Gardens — free, warm glasshouses, duck pond
Riccarton House & Bush — free native bush walk and weekend farmers' market

sections.heading: Hot Pools and Snow Within Reach
sections.body:
Hanmer Springs is the classic Canterbury winter day trip — about 90 minutes north, with hot pools, hydroslides, a warm lazy river and a toddler pool, often with snow on the surrounding hills. Entry is around $28/adult, $16/child.

For snow, Mt Hutt is roughly 90 minutes away and has a good kids' ski school; Porters is closer (about an hour) and great for beginners. Even if you don't ski, a snow-play day is a memorable winter outing — bring waterproof gloves and snow gear.
list: Hanmer Springs Thermal Pools — ~90 min north, hydroslides, lazy river, toddler pool
Mt Hutt — strong kids' ski school, ~90 min from the city
Porters Alpine Resort — closest field, great for beginners and snow play
Tekapo Springs (day trip) — hot pools, ice rink and snow tube park with mountain views

sections.heading: Rainy-Day Backups
sections.body: For a wet Canterbury day, the city has plenty of warm, dry options beyond the museums. Trampoline parks, bowling, indoor climbing and the leisure pools all keep kids busy out of the weather.
list: Trampoline parks — Flip Out, Jump and others, ~$20/child/hour
Clip 'n Climb Christchurch — colourful indoor climbing for ages 4+
Jellie Park and Pioneer leisure pools — warm pools and hydroslides
Ten-pin bowling and the cinema — cheap holiday kids' sessions

sections.heading: A Sample Winter Holiday Day
sections.body: A great mixed day: start at the free Air Force Museum at Wigram when it opens, where the kids can climb into cockpits and try the simulators, then warm up with a packed lunch. In the afternoon, head to Alpine Ice for a public skating session, finishing at Tūranga library to thaw out and read. Total spend for a family of four: around $80, mostly the skating.

sections.heading: What It Costs
sections.body: Christchurch is easy on the wallet thanks to its free flagship attractions, with the Antarctic Centre, wildlife parks and Hanmer Springs the bigger spends. Here's a rough guide for a family of four.

cta.text: Compare car & campervan hire for your Christchurch trip

faq.question: When are the Christchurch school holidays in winter 2026?
faq.answer: Christchurch schools follow the national Term 2 break, Saturday 27 June to Sunday 12 July 2026, with Term 3 starting Monday 13 July. Check your own school's calendar for any minor variations.

faq.question: What free things can families do in Christchurch in winter?
faq.answer: The Air Force Museum, Christchurch Art Gallery and Canterbury Museum are free, the Botanic Gardens and Margaret Mahy Playground are free, and Tūranga central library has five floors of free play and maker spaces.

faq.question: Is the International Antarctic Centre worth it in winter?
faq.answer: Yes — it's an all-indoor experience, so winter is ideal. The storm room, little blue penguins, aquarium and Hägglunds ride fill two to three hours. Book online for cheaper combo tickets; entry is around $59/adult, $39/child.

faq.question: Where can families find snow and hot pools near Christchurch?
faq.answer: Hanmer Springs (about 90 minutes north) has hot pools and hydroslides, often with snow on the hills. For skiing or snow play, Mt Hutt is around 90 minutes and Porters is closer at about an hour, both with good beginner terrain.

faq.question: What can we do in Christchurch on a rainy winter day?
faq.answer: The free museums and Tūranga library are great starts, plus trampoline parks, Clip 'n Climb, the Jellie Park and Pioneer leisure pools, bowling and the cinema all keep kids busy and dry.
